create.title: Uretim Transfer Emri Olustur

guide.title: Transfer Rehberi
guide.subtitle: Bu ekran PT tarafinda hangi rol ile neden acildigini netlestirir.
guide.materialSupplyBody: Uretim tuketimi icin gerekli hammaddeleri besleme alanina cekmek icin kullanilir.
guide.semiFinishedMoveBody: Bir emrin ciktisini sonraki emrin tuketimine veya istasyonuna tasir.
guide.outputMoveBody: Tamamlanan urunun uretimden mamul depoya cikisini yonetir.

create.editSubtitle: Tamamlanmamis ve henüz islenmemis transfer emirlerini burada guncelleyebilirsiniz.
create.cloneSubtitle: Var olan transfer emrini baz alip yeni bir transfer emri olusturuyorsunuz.
create.subtitle: Uretim planina bagli hammadde besleme, yari mamul tasima veya mamul cikis akislarini tanimlayin.
create.editModeBody: Bu transfer daha once kaydedildi. Burada yaptiginiz degisiklikler mevcut transfer emrini gunceller; yeni bir kopya olusturmaz.
create.cloneModeBody: Bu ekran mevcut transfer emrini baz alir. Belge no yeni olmalidir; kaydettiginizde yeni bir transfer emri olustururuz.

info.overviewBody: Bu ekran, uretim planindaki bir asamaya malzeme beslemek, ara mamulu sonraki istasyona tasimak veya tamamlanan urunu mamul depoya almak icin transfer emri olusturur. Oneri paneli, secili plan veya emrin ihtiyacindan otomatik satir uretir.
info.linkBody: Plan veya emir baglantisi kuruldugunda sistem, bu transferin hangi uretim ihtiyacini karşıladığını bilir. Boylece hangi tuketim satirinin beslendigi veya hangi ciktinin tasindigi izlenebilir olur.
info.linesBody: Once onerileri getirerek sistemin uretim ihtiyacindan tavsiye uretmesini saglayin. Sonra gerekli satirlari secip mevcut taslaga ekleyin veya degistirin. Bu ekran planlamaciya malzeme besleme ve ara istasyon hareketlerini kontrollu kurdurmak icin tasarlandi.

info.overviewBody: Bu ekran once ana urunu ve miktari secmenizi, sonra uretimi asamalara bolmenizi, en son da hangi asamanin once veya ayni anda ilerleyecegini belirlemenizi saglar. Kayit aninda sistem plani uretim emirlerine cevirir.
info.headerBody: Ana urun, planin sonunda elde etmek istediginiz mamuldur. Ana yapkod bu mamulun varyantini anlatir. Hazir bir is emri veya stok bilgisi varsa hizli giris ile bu alanlari otomatik doldurabilirsiniz.
info.stageBody: Her kart tek bir uretim asamasidir. Ornek: govde hazirlama, montaj, paketleme. Seri modda bir asama bitmeden sonraki baslamaz. Paralel veya karma modda ayni sequence numarasina sahip asamalar birlikte ilerleyebilir.
info.outputBody: Cikti, ilgili asama sonunda elde edilen urundur. Ara mamul olabilir, nihai mamul olabilir. Burada planlanan miktar yazilir; gercekte ne kadar uretildigi process ekraninda kaydedilir.
info.consumptionBody: Tuketim, bu asamayi tamamlamak icin gereken malzemedir. Planlama ekraninda neyin gerekli oldugu yazilir. Hangi seri veya lotun kullanildigi ise daha sonra operator tarafinda process ekraninda girilir veya ERP/Netsis tarafindan dogrulanir.
info.flowBody: Akis, hangi asamanin once bitecegini ve hangisinin onu bekleyecegini belirler. Bu alan sayesinde birlestirme veya son montaj gibi asamalar, gerekli onceki asamalar bitmeden baslatilmaz.

Trendyol.title: Where do I get Trendyol credentials?
Trendyol.summary: Trendyol uses Basic Auth. Supplier ID, API Key and API Secret are used for product, price, stock and order services.
steps: Sign in to Seller Panel with a master or authorized user.
Open Account Information > Integration Information and copy Supplier ID, API Key and API Secret.
Enter Supplier ID in Seller / Store No and enter API Key/API Secret in the credential fields.
Keep the product, price, stock and order operations enabled as needed.
Trendyol.setupSummary: Supplier ID, API Key and API Secret are available in the Trendyol seller panel under Account Information > Integration Information. PROD/STAGE credentials may differ.

credentialHelp.supplierId: The supplier/seller number shown in Trendyol Integration Information.
credentialHelp.apiKey: Trendyol API key visible to the master/authorized user.
credentialHelp.apiSecret: Secret key used together with API Key for Basic Auth.

Trendyol.connectionModeHelp: Trendyol uses Basic Auth in this integration; Supplier ID and API Key/API Secret must be filled together or authentication fails.
connectionChecklist: Get Supplier ID, API Key and API Secret from Trendyol seller integration settings.
Separate production and sandbox environments to avoid URL and token confusion.
Validate catalog, stock and price mappings before publishing products.
Run one small test SKU end-to-end before production release.

Hepsiburada.title: Where do I get Hepsiburada credentials?
Hepsiburada.summary: Hepsiburada requires merchant/store information and API keys. Operations usually run with batch or transaction tracking.
steps: Prepare Hepsiburada developer account and merchant panel access.
Confirm the merchant/store ID in merchant panel.
Create API Key/API Secret from the developer portal or integrator authorization screen.
Enter Merchant ID in Seller / Store No and keys in the Credentials tab.
Hepsiburada.setupSummary: Developer account, API keys and merchant/store information are prepared from the Hepsiburada developer/merchant panel. Integrator authorization is managed in the merchant panel.

credentialHelp.merchantId: Unique merchant/store id from Hepsiburada merchant panel.
credentialHelp.apiKey: Client/API key from developer portal or authorized integrator account.
credentialHelp.apiSecret: Secret used for token generation or Basic/Auth flow.

connectionChecklist: Verify developer account and merchant panel access with required API scopes.
Confirm Merchant ID and API key/secret pair belong to the same seller context.
Activate order and catalog permissions for the integration.
If callbacks are enabled, validate HTTPS callback URL and headers.

N11.title: Where do I get n11 credentials?
N11.summary: n11 REST API works with appKey/appSecret. Price and stock updates create a taskId; results are tracked by the sync job.
steps: Log in to the n11 seller panel with an authorized user.
Get App Key and App Secret from the API/integration information area.
Enter Seller ID in Seller / Store No and App Key/App Secret in credential fields.
Track task results after price or stock updates.
N11.setupSummary: AppKey/appSecret values are obtained from the n11 seller panel. Price-stock updates create a taskId and the result is tracked by the Hangfire job.

credentialHelp.sellerId: n11 store/seller id. Used as the store identifier in channel mapping.
credentialHelp.appKey: Application key obtained from n11 seller panel for REST API access.
credentialHelp.appSecret: Secret key used together with appKey for n11 REST API access.

Pazarama.title: Where do I get Pazarama credentials?
Pazarama.summary: Pazarama integration obtains a token with clientId/clientSecret. Product, price-stock and order services run in the seller channel context.
steps: Complete Pazarama seller application and integration authorization.
Get Client ID and Client Secret from the seller panel or integration team.
Enter Seller ID in Seller / Store No and Client ID/Client Secret in credential fields.
Queue product, price and stock operations and track results from the job screen.
Pazarama.setupSummary: Pazarama integration obtains a token with clientId/clientSecret; product, price-stock and order operations run by seller channel.

credentialHelp.sellerId: Pazarama seller/store id. Used as store context in channel and token requests.
credentialHelp.clientId: Client id provided after Pazarama integration application.
credentialHelp.clientSecret: Secret key used in the Pazarama token flow.

PttAvm.title: Where do I get PTT AVM credentials?
PttAvm.summary: PTT AVM catalog integration works with products/upsert. Product, price and stock submissions are tracked with trackingid.
steps: Enable API access from the PTT AVM developer or seller integration panel.
Get API Key and Token values.
Enter Seller ID in Seller / Store No and API Key/Token in credential fields.
Track the trackingid result from the Hangfire monitoring screen after product, price or stock submission.
PttAvm.warning: In the PTT AVM upsert flow, missing variants or missing fields can affect existing product data. Test catalog mapper fields before production.
PttAvm.setupSummary: PTT AVM catalog integration works with products/upsert. Product, price and stock submissions are tracked with trackingid.

credentialHelp.apiKey: API key obtained from the PTT AVM developer/integration panel.
credentialHelp.token: Access value used as Bearer token in PTT AVM API requests.

Idefix.title: Where do I get İdefix credentials?
Idefix.summary: İdefix product integration works with vendorId, API key and secret. Product creation and stock/price submissions are tracked through batch results.
steps: Prepare API access from the İdefix developer or seller integration panel.
Get Vendor ID, API Key and API Secret values.
Enter Vendor ID in Seller / Store No and API Key/API Secret in credential fields.
Track batch results after product creation and stock/price submissions.
Idefix.setupSummary: İdefix product integration works with vendorId, API key and secret. Product creation and stock/price submissions are tracked through batch results.

credentialHelp.vendorId: İdefix seller/vendor id. Used as path parameter in product and inventory endpoints.
credentialHelp.apiKey: API key obtained from the İdefix developer panel.
credentialHelp.apiSecret: Secret key used together with API Key for Basic/Auth flow.

Ciceksepeti.title: Where do I get Çiçeksepeti credentials?
Ciceksepeti.summary: Çiçeksepeti marketplace API credentials are obtained from the seller panel or integration team. Product, price, stock and order operations are managed with channel path settings.
steps: Complete Çiçeksepeti seller application and marketplace integration authorization.
Get API Key and optional Token from the seller panel or integration team.
Enter Seller ID in Seller / Store No and API Key/Token in credential fields.
Track product, price and stock submission results from the job monitoring screen.
Ciceksepeti.warning: Çiçeksepeti public documentation is limited. Confirm live endpoint paths from the seller panel and update provider options before production.
Ciceksepeti.setupSummary: Çiçeksepeti marketplace API credentials are obtained from the seller panel or integration team. Product, price, stock and order operations are managed with provider path settings.

credentialHelp.apiKey: API key obtained from Çiçeksepeti seller panel or integration team.
credentialHelp.token: Optional access value if the channel requires Bearer token.

Ebay.title: Where do I get eBay credentials?
Ebay.summary: eBay Sell Inventory API publishes products through inventory item, offer and publish steps. Price and stock use the bulk update price quantity endpoint.
steps: Prepare an eBay Developer account and Sell Inventory API permissions.
Generate an access token with Inventory API scopes from seller authorization.
Select Marketplace ID according to the target country. Examples: EBAY_US, EBAY_GB, EBAY_DE.
Complete eBay category, merchant location and listing policy values in catalog attributes before publishing products.
Save Seller ID, Marketplace ID and Access Token on this screen.
Ebay.warning: eBay product publish requires category, merchant location key and listing policy values. The API rejects product creation when these values are missing.
Ebay.setupSummary: eBay Sell Inventory API publishes products through inventory item, offer and publish steps. Price and stock use the bulk update price quantity endpoint.

credentialHelp.marketplaceId: eBay marketplace code for the target country. Examples: EBAY_US, EBAY_GB, EBAY_DE.
credentialHelp.accessToken: OAuth access token generated with Sell Inventory API scopes.

Ebay.connectionModeHelp: Most eBay flows require an OAuth access token with Inventory scope; seller and marketplace context should be sent if the listing uses country-specific marketplaces.
connectionChecklist: Complete OAuth token flow in eBay Developer Console for Inventory scope.
Confirm marketplace and seller context for each publish request.
Populate category, merchant location and policy values before publishing.
Run a sandbox or non-critical test offer and confirm response IDs and offer status.

Shopify.title: Where do I get Shopify credentials?
Shopify.summary: Shopify Admin API works with product create, variant price update and inventory_levels/set stock update flows.
steps: Create a custom app or admin app in Shopify admin and enable Admin API permissions.
Generate an Admin API access token with Products and Inventory permissions.
Save the Shop Domain with the myshopify.com domain.
Find the Location ID that will be used for stock updates.
Save Shop Domain, Admin Access Token, API Version and Location ID on this screen.
Shopify.warning: Shopify stock updates use inventory item id and location id, not variant id. This mapping is stored after product creation.
Shopify.setupSummary: Shopify Admin API works with product create, variant price update and inventory_levels/set stock update flows.

credentialHelp.accessToken: Admin API access token with Products and Inventory permissions.
credentialHelp.apiVersion: Shopify Admin API version to use. Example: 2026-01.
credentialHelp.locationId: Shopify location id used for stock updates.

WooCommerce.title: Where do I get WooCommerce credentials?
WooCommerce.summary: WooCommerce REST API uses Consumer Key/Secret for product create, price update and stock update operations through the products endpoint.
steps: Open WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API in WordPress admin.
Create Read/Write Consumer Key and Consumer Secret credentials.
Save Store URL with the HTTPS site address.
Save Store URL, Consumer Key, Consumer Secret and API Version on this screen.
WooCommerce.warning: WooCommerce price and stock updates use product id. For variable products, store variation id mapping inside the listing id.
WooCommerce.setupSummary: WooCommerce REST API uses Consumer Key/Secret for product create, price update and stock update operations through the products endpoint.

credentialHelp.storeUrl: WooCommerce store base URL. Example: https://store.com.
credentialHelp.apiVersion: REST API version to use. Example: wc/v3.

AdobeCommerce.title: Where do I get Adobe Commerce credentials?
AdobeCommerce.summary: Adobe Commerce/Magento REST API uses an access token for product create, price update and MSI source item stock sync.
steps: Create an Integration in Adobe Commerce admin and grant Catalog and Inventory permissions.
Copy the Access Token after activating the integration.
Select Store Code for REST paths. Most installations use default.
Select Source Code for MSI stock sync.
Save Base URL, Access Token, Store Code and Source Code on this screen.
AdobeCommerce.warning: Adobe Commerce stock update uses MSI source items. In multi-warehouse/source setups, sourceCode should be managed with ERP warehouse mapping.
AdobeCommerce.setupSummary: Adobe Commerce/Magento REST API uses an access token for product create, price update and MSI source item stock sync.

credentialHelp.baseUrl: Adobe Commerce/Magento store base URL. Example: https://store.com.
credentialHelp.accessToken: Bearer token obtained from Integration or admin token flow.
credentialHelp.storeCode: Store code used inside REST path. Example: default.
credentialHelp.sourceCode: MSI source code used for stock sync. Example: default.

Amazon.title: Where do I get Amazon SP-API credentials?
Amazon.summary: Amazon is the most comprehensive flow. Seller Central developer profile, app registration, LWA OAuth and AWS SigV4 credentials are required together.
steps: Complete developer profile and SP-API app registration in Seller Central.
Get Login with Amazon client id and secret for the app.
Generate a refresh token from seller authorization.
Prepare AWS IAM/SigV4 credentials and Role ARN if needed.
Choose Marketplace ID and region according to the target country.
Amazon.warning: PROD/SANDBOX and marketplace region selection are critical for Amazon. Requests can fail even with a valid signature if region or marketplace ID is wrong.
Amazon.setupSummary: Amazon SP-API requires developer profile, app registration, Seller Central authorization, LWA refresh token and AWS SigV4 credentials together.

credentialHelp.sellerId: Merchant/seller identifier of the Seller Central account.
credentialHelp.marketplaceId: Amazon marketplace identifier for the target country; verify from Amazon Marketplace IDs documentation.
credentialHelp.region: SP-API endpoint region. Turkey/Europe typically uses the EU endpoint family.
credentialHelp.lwaClientId: Login with Amazon client id created after app registration.
credentialHelp.refreshToken: Long-lived refresh token received after seller authorization.
credentialHelp.awsAccessKey: IAM access key used for SP-API request signing.
credentialHelp.awsSecretKey: IAM secret key used for SP-API request signing.
credentialHelp.roleArn: Role ARN if IAM role based access is used.

Amazon.connectionModeHelp: Amazon SP-API requires LWA OAuth + AWS Signature Version 4 together. Seller ID and region must match the target marketplace before sending requests.
connectionChecklist: Create SP-API application and get LWA client credentials in Seller Central.
Complete seller authorization flow and obtain refresh token.
Validate AWS IAM access keys and Signature V4 region pairing.
Confirm marketplace IDs and endpoint region for each sales destination.
Validate listing payload fields (prices, quantities, units) before production.

Etsy.title: Where do I get Etsy credentials?
Etsy.summary: Etsy Open API v3 uses OAuth. App registration provides keystring/shared secret and shop access is authorized through OAuth scopes.
steps: Create an app in Etsy Developer portal.
Copy API keystring and shared secret.
Complete OAuth authorization for the shop and generate a refresh token.
Save Shop ID and token values in this screen.
Etsy.setupSummary: Etsy app registration provides API keystring/shared secret; shop access is authorized through OAuth token scopes.

credentialHelp.refreshToken: Refresh token received after OAuth authorization.

employee.user: This links the PPE card to a system user. First name and last name are filled from the selected user.
employee.customer: Select the customer/company the employee belongs to. This is shown on documents and reports.
employee.employeeCode: Enter the internal personnel or registry code used to identify the employee.
employee.department: Select the employee's department. The role list is filtered by this selection.
employee.role: Select the employee's actual role definition. PPE entitlement is calculated from role plus group code.
employee.employmentStartDate: Initial and 3-month entitlements are calculated from this date. A wrong date means a wrong entitlement result.
employee.qrCode: Unique QR value scanned in the field to select the employee quickly.

matrix.department: Select the department this entitlement rule belongs to.
matrix.role: Select the role definition the entitlement belongs to. The system always resolves entitlement by role and group code.
matrix.groupCode: The main key of this screen is the group code. Entitlement matching runs by group, not by stock code. Different stocks in the same group share the same rule; if a stock code exists, treat it as informational only.
matrix.initialQuantity: Total quantity the employee can receive for this group when first joining.
matrix.initialFrequencyDays: Frequency controls whether the entitlement is taken all at once or in parts. If you enter 30, the employee must wait at least 30 days after one issue before another issue from the same entitlement. Leave empty if no day-gap rule is needed.
matrix.initialBulk: When enabled, the employee may receive the full initial entitlement in one issue. When disabled, the system forces partial usage and applies the frequency-day rule if defined.
matrix.initialFrequencyQuantity: Maximum quantity allowed in one issue or in each frequency window. Example: if total entitlement is 3 and frequency quantity is 1, the employee can receive at most 1 item each time.
matrix.threeMonthQuantity: Extra entitlement that becomes available 3 months after employment start.
matrix.threeMonthFrequencyDays: If the 3-month entitlement will be used in parts, this defines how many days must pass between issues.
matrix.threeMonthBulk: When enabled, the full 3-month entitlement can be taken in one issue. Otherwise the system allows only partial usage.
matrix.threeMonthFrequencyQuantity: If the 3-month entitlement is used in parts, this defines the maximum quantity per issue.
matrix.routineQuantity: Standard quantity granted again in every recurring period after the initial and 3-month phases are completed.
matrix.routinePeriodType: Defines the time unit used to renew the recurring entitlement. Usually day, month, or year.
matrix.routinePeriodInterval: Defines how often the recurring entitlement renews. Example: year + 1 means every year; day + 30 means every 30 days.
matrix.routineFrequencyDays: If the recurring entitlement is used in parts, this defines how many days must pass between issues.
matrix.routineBulk: When enabled, the full recurring entitlement can be taken in one issue. Otherwise the system enforces partial usage.
matrix.routineFrequencyQuantity: If recurring entitlement is used in parts, this defines the maximum quantity per issue.
matrix.isActive: Inactive rows are ignored by entitlement calculation.
matrix.isMandatory: Enable this if the group is mandatory for occupational safety; it can be used in controls and reporting.
